description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with three stages (docker, build, deploy) and a global image registry.gitlab.com/taichunmin/docker-compose-git:latest. In the build stage add a build-book job that uses the image registry.gitlab.com/taichunmin/progit2-zh-tw:latest, runs the commands: mkdir images; cp book/*/images/* images/; asciidoctor progit.asc || true; asciidoctor-pdf -r asciidoctor-pdf-cjk-kai_gen_gothic -a pdf-style=KaiGenGothicTW progit.asc || true; asciidoctor-epub3 progit.asc || true; asciidoctor-epub3 -a ebook-format=kf8 progit.asc || true; mkdir public; cp progit.{pdf,epub} progit-kf8.epub public/ || true; cp progit.html public/index.html; cp -r images/ public/; ls public, and is tagged with docker. In the docker stage add a build-docker job that runs only on the web ref, uses the docker:dind service, and executes docker login -u $CI_REGISTRY_USER -p $CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD $CI_REGISTRY; docker-compose -f docker-build.yml build; docker-compose -f docker-build.yml push; docker-compose -f docker-build.yml down --rmi all, also tagged docker. Cache the public/ directory across jobs with key $CI_PIPELINE_ID. In the deploy stage add a pages job that runs only on the master branch, uses the global image, runs ls public, and publishes the public/ folder as a GitLab Pages artifact (paths: public, when: always).
